title: It's only me
scene: 妻子害怕独自看牙医，丈夫不得不开车送她去诊所，在花园里等候。她在候诊室里紧张得直发抖；丈夫到外面抽烟时，她忽然想起钱包落在外面，从后门冲出来。与此同时丈夫敲错了窗户想借火，大喊「是我！」——却把妻子和牙医都吓了一跳。他进错了花园，毫无紧急理由却把大家都吓坏了。本课语法重点是 have (got) to 表客观不得不，以及 remember/forget 后接不定式与动名词的区别。

words.eg: She trembled so much in the waiting room that the nurse felt sorry for her.
Shivering in their wet clothes, the teenagers looked on while three men dived repeatedly into the water to locate the dish.
Though Verrazano is by no means considered to be a great explorer, his name will probably remain immortal,
The Verrazano Bridge, which was designed by Othmar Ammann, joins Brooklyn to Staten Island.
The Verrazano Bridge, which was designed by Othmar Ammann, joins Brooklyn to Staten Island.
It has a span of 4, 260 feet.
Each cable was made of tens of thousands of steel wires. 每根缆索由数万根钢丝绞成。
The towers are built on immense underwater platforms made of steel and concrete.
They support the cables from which the bridge has been suspended.
Engineers had to estimate the immensity of the forces acting on such a length.
Engineers had to estimate the immensity of the forces acting on such a length.
It has been estimated that if the bridge were packed with cars, it would still only be carrying a third of its total capacity.
Despite its immensity, it is both simple and elegant,
Despite its immensity, it is both simple and elegant,
fulfilling its designer's dream to creat 'an enormous object drawn as faintly as possible'.
The works belonged to a young artist who believed that current could be part of art.
Modern sculpture rarely surprises us any more.
The idea that modern art can only be seen in museums is mistaken.
The first thing I saw when I entered the art gallery was a notice which said: 'Do not touch the exhibits, some of them are dangerous!'
How might some of the exhibits have been dangerous?
Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move in response to a gust of wind are quite familiar to everybody.
The sides of the dish were so smooth that it was almost impossible to attach hawsers and chains to the rim without damaging it.
Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres.
The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time.
Suet pudding is enough to repel many visitors from abroad. 羊油布丁足以令许多外国游客却步。
These lights flickered continuously like traffic lights which have gone mad.

grammar.explain: have to / have got to 表客观情势迫使「不得不」，叙述过去用 had to；否定 don't have to 表「不必」，与 mustn't「禁止」不同。易错点：have to 无完成时，用 had to 表过去。
examples: I had to drive her to the clinic because she was terrified. （她害怕独自前往，我不得不开车送她去诊所。）
You don't have to wait outside if you don't want to. （你若不愿意，不必在外面等。）

grammar.explain: remember/forget + to do 指记得/忘记去做（未做或提醒将来）；+ doing 指记得/忘记做过（已完成）。易错点：leaving 表「把……落下」这一已发生的事。
examples: She remembered leaving her wallet on the seat. （她想起把钱包落在座位上了。）
Don't forget to call the dentist. （别忘了给牙医打电话。）

tips: ①客观迫使用 have to，过去 had to；②不必用 don't have to，禁止用 mustn't；③remember doing 表记得做过，remember to do 表记得去做。

title: A noble gangster
scene: 邻居赫斯特伍德穿着粗布衣服、开着旧卡车，陌生人觉得他穷，了解他的人却知道他曾是一带最有势力的匪徒，控制着大片地盘。虽已金盆洗手，他仍习惯于奢侈生活，公寓里摆满名画和红酒。他说人总得有自己的标准，警察不再监视他，但他的过去从未被遗忘。本课语法重点是 used to 表过去习惯与 be used to doing 表习惯于。

words.eg: He used to be the most powerful gangster in the district and once controlled a large territory.
Though he has retired from that life, he is used to living in great luxury.
He used to be the most powerful gangster in the district and once controlled a large territory.
How might some of the exhibits have been dangerous?
Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move in response to a gust of wind are quite familiar to everybody.
The sides of the dish were so smooth that it was almost impossible to attach hawsers and chains to the rim without damaging it.
Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres.
The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time.
Suet pudding is enough to repel many visitors from abroad. 羊油布丁足以令许多外国游客却步。
These lights flickered continuously like traffic lights which have gone mad.
This is a major achievement because, in relative terms, planets are very small and do not emit light.
Sparks were emitted from small black boxes and red lamps flashed on and off angrily.
It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.
It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.
These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!
These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!
These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!
A very dear cat
She withdrew ￡1000 from her bank and followed the kidnapper's instructions.
Kidnappers are rarely interested in animals, but they recently took considerable interest in Mrs. Eleanor Ramsay's cat.
Mrs. Eleanor Ramsay, a very wealthy old lady, has shared a flat with her cat, Rastus, for a great many years.
Rastus leads an orderly life.
Three days after Rastus' disappearance, Mrs. Ramsay received an anonymous letter.
Three days after Rastus' disappearance, Mrs. Ramsay received an anonymous letter.
The writer stated that Rastus was in safe hands and would be returned immediately if Mrs. Ramsay paid a ransom of ￡1, 000.
Mrs.Ramsay was instructed to place the money in a cardboard box and to leave it outside her door.

grammar.explain: 本课重点语法：used to。请结合课文与题库理解用法。
examples: （见课文相关例句。）
（见题库与句型转换练习。）

grammar.explain: be used to doing 表「习惯于」，to 是介词后接动名词；get used to 表逐渐习惯。易错点：不要与 used to do 混淆。
examples: He is used to living in luxury. （他习惯于奢侈的生活。）
She has got used to being alone. （她已逐渐习惯独处。）

tips: ①过去习惯现已不再用 used to do，否定 didn't use to；②习惯于用 be used to doing；③would 表过去反复动作但不用于 be。

title: Fifty pence worth of trouble
scene: 小男孩在糖果店付钱时，五十便士硬币掉进狭缝。缝隙不够宽，手伸不进去，他趴在地上不肯离开。店员不耐烦报了警，警察撬开缝隙才取回硬币，还训斥他为这点钱惹麻烦。本课语法重点是 enough...to 与 so/such...that 结果结构。

words.eg: Mrs. Eleanor Ramsay, a very wealthy old lady, has shared a flat with her cat, Rastus, for a great many years.
Rastus leads an orderly life.
Three days after Rastus' disappearance, Mrs. Ramsay received an anonymous letter.
Three days after Rastus' disappearance, Mrs. Ramsay received an anonymous letter.
The writer stated that Rastus was in safe hands and would be returned immediately if Mrs. Ramsay paid a ransom of ￡1, 000.
Mrs.Ramsay was instructed to place the money in a cardboard box and to leave it outside her door.
Goods that fail to meet standards must be withdrawn from sale. 不合格商品必须下架停售。
Sure enough, Rastus arrived punctually at seven o'clock that evening.
The police were astounded when Mrs. Ramsay told them what she had done.
Pioneer pilots

words.cn: 对(英国)贵族的尊称；勋爵
words.eg: In 1908 Lord Northcliffe offered a prize of ￡1, 000 to the first man who would fly across the English Channel.
Two days later, Louis Bleriot arrived near Calais with a plane called 'No.XI'.
A week before, he had completed a successful overland flight during which he covered twenty-six miles.
Boxing matches were very popular in England two hundred years ago.
This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.
In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money.

words.cn: 职业拳击手(尤指古时赤手拳击手)
words.eg: for there were no rules and a prizefighter could be seriously injured or even killed during a match.
However, boxing was very crude,
The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules.
Though he was technically a prizefighter,
Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.
In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity.
He was adored by rich and poor alike.
He was adored by rich and poor alike.
Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old.
This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.

grammar.explain: 形容词/副词 + enough + to do 表「足够……可以/以至于能」。易错点：foolish enough to do 表「蠢到去做……」。
examples: The gap was not wide enough for his hand to reach through. （缝隙不够宽，手伸不过去。）
He was foolish enough to argue over fifty pence. （他蠢到为五十便士争辩。）

grammar.explain: so + 形容词/副词 + that；such + (a) + 形容词 + 名词 + that，均表「如此……以至于……」。易错点：such 后接名词，so 后不接名词。
examples: She trembled so much that the nurse felt sorry for her. （她紧张得发抖，护士都替她难过。）
It was such a narrow gap that the policeman had to pry it open. （缝隙太窄，警察只好撬开。）

tips: ①形容词 + enough + to do；②so + 形容词 + that 接从句；③such + a + 形容词 + 名词 + that。

title: Mary had a little lamb
scene: 农民的女儿玛丽把羔羊当宠物，它习惯了在农场到处跟着她，甚至跟进教室。老师不得不把它领出去，校长最终允许它待在草地上，但玛丽必须在课间照料并承担责任。本课语法重点是 be accustomed to doing 与 have to 表客观义务。

words.eg: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules.
Though he was technically a prizefighter,
Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.
In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity.
He was adored by rich and poor alike.
He was adored by rich and poor alike.
Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old.
This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.
The two men quarrelled bitterly and it was clear that the argument could only be settled by a fight.
The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated.
Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful Academy and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils.
Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt.
He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts and died in poverty in 1836.
Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end.
In many ways, this is unfortunate for the poor actors who are required to go on repeating the same lines night after night.
The works belonged to a young artist who believed that current could be part of art.
One would expect them to know their parts by heart and never have cause to falter.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler?
However, he spent money freely and was imprisoned more than once for debt.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler?
One night, the gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if,
The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.
The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.

grammar.explain: be accustomed to doing = be used to doing，表习惯于。易错点：to 后接动名词，不接动词原形。
examples: The lamb was accustomed to following her everywhere. （羔羊习惯了到处跟着她。）
Mary had to tend the animal during breaks. （玛丽必须在课间照料它。）

grammar.explain: have to 表客观义务「必须/不得不」，过去式 had to。易错点：与 must 不同，have to 强调外部情势迫使。
examples: Mary had to tend the animal during breaks. （玛丽必须在课间照料它。）
The teacher was obliged to lead it outside. （老师不得不把它领出去。）

tips: ①be accustomed to doing = be used to doing；②had to 表客观必须；③to 后接动名词。

title: The longest suspension bridge in the world
scene: 韦拉扎诺海峡大桥连接斯塔滕岛与布鲁克林，竣工时主跨为世界最长。四根巨型钢缆悬吊桥面，工程师须估算巨大力量；自一九六四年以来大桥每天承载海量车流，成为纽约天际线地标。本课语法重点是现在完成时与 since/for，以及长度、容量等数量表达。

words.eg: When it was completed, its main span of 4,260 feet was the longest in the world.
Since 1964 the bridge has carried a huge daily capacity of traffic.
Engineers had to estimate the immensity of the forces acting on such a length.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler?
However, he spent money freely and was imprisoned more than once for debt.
A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler?
One night, the gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if,
The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.
The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.
The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.
It was simply a blank sheet of paper.
Then, squinting his eyes, he said: 'The light is dim. Read the letter to me.'
Then, squinting his eyes, he said: 'The light is dim. Read the letter to me.'
the gaoler replied: 'The light is indeed dim, sire.  I must get my glasses.  '
the gaoler returned a few moments later with a pair of glasses and the usual copy of the letter which he proceeded to read to the prisoner.
One man's meat is another man's poison
People become quite illogical when they try to decide what can be eaten and what cannot be eaten.
If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy.
If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy.

words.cn: 令人反感的，令人生厌的
words.eg: You would not be able to understand why some people find it repulsive.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.

grammar.explain: have/has done + since/for 表从过去持续到现在。since 接时间点，for 接时间段。易错点：since 从句用一般过去时，主句用现在完成时。
examples: The bridge has carried traffic since 1964. （自一九六四年以来大桥一直承载车流。）
She has been open to the public ever since. （此后一直对公众开放。）

grammar.explain: 描述长度、跨度、容量等用 of 短语或数字 + 单位。易错点：feet 是 foot 的复数表长度单位。
examples: Its main span of 4,260 feet was the longest in the world. （其四二千二百六十英尺的主跨当时世界最长。）
Each cable was made of tens of thousands of steel wires. （每根缆索由数万根钢丝绞成。）

tips: ①现在完成时 + since/for；②数量与尺寸表达。

title: Electric currents in modern art
scene: 现代艺术画廊举办电线雕塑与电灯展览，一名学生接错线路引燃材料，消防队控制住火势前多件展品受损。画廊老板称珍品所剩无几，学生辩称风险属于每一种新的表达形式。本课语法重点是 possess/belong to 等所属关系表达。

words.eg: The works belonged to a young artist who believed that current could be part of art.
A modern art gallery opened an unusual exhibition of wire sculptures and electric lights.
A modern art gallery opened an unusual exhibition of wire sculptures and electric lights.
If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy.

words.cn: 令人反感的，令人生厌的
words.eg: You would not be able to understand why some people find it repulsive.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail.
No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail.
Though he has retired from that life, he is used to living in great luxury.
There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food.
My friend, Robert, lives in a country where snails are despised.
In spite of national differences, certain funny situations have a universal appeal.
The idea never appealed to me very much, but one day, after a heavy shower,
I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails taking a stroll on some of my prize plants.
Acting on a sudden impulse, I collected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert.
Acting on a sudden impulse, I collected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert.

We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
but I could not conceal the fact that I was impressed by the size of the diamonds.
The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.

words.cn: 令人激动的，扣人心弦的
words.eg: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.

grammar.explain: possess 较正式表「拥有」；belong to 表「属于」。易错点：belong to 不用被动。
examples: The works belonged to a young artist. （作品属于一位年轻艺术家。）
The gallery possessed only a few valuable pieces. （画廊仅有几件珍品。）

grammar.explain: belong to / be the property of 表所属。易错点：所有格与 of 结构按正式程度选用。
examples: The coin was all the property he possessed. （那枚硬币是他仅有的财产。）
Risk belongs to every new form of expression. （风险属于每一种新的表达形式。）

tips: ①possess / belong to；②所属关系表达。

title: A very dear cat
scene: 史密斯太太的名贵波斯猫失踪后，她接到匿名勒索电话，警告最好不要报警。她按指示送去赎金，当晚猫安然送还，后来发现窃贼偷猫前把屋子翻了个遍。她说最好给猫戴上地址项圈。本课语法重点是过去完成时与 had better 劝告结构。

words.eg: Two days later she received an anonymous call demanding a large ransom.
Two days later she received an anonymous call demanding a large ransom.
A skeleton in the cupboard
We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
but I could not conceal the fact that I was impressed by the size of the diamonds.
The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.

words.cn: 令人激动的，扣人心弦的
words.eg: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.
His story is still told as a lesson in talent, fame and ruin.
The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine,
It is all very well for such things to occur in fiction.
To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn,
George studied medicine in his youth.
George showed me to the guestroom which, he said, was rarely used.
He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner.
After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.
After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.
Last month, our neighbour's puppy chewed up the savings that had been hidden in a drawer.
I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it petrified.
A skeleton was dangling before my eyes.
The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.

words.cn: 不表同情的，无动于衷的
words.eg: But George was unsympathetic.
'That's Sebastian. You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.'
She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past.
Before they were replaced by steamships,
Workers had been restoring the old vessel for months when a sudden blaze broke out.

grammar.explain: 海难叙事中，“出事之前已经发生的事”用过去完成时，如 had been described、had set out。易错点：主线事件（相撞、下沉）用一般过去时，只有更早一层的动作才用 had done。
examples: The liner had been described as unsinkable before she set out. （启航之前，这艘班轮曾被描述为永不沉没。）
By the time the lifeboats were lowered, the ship had begun to sink. （放下救生艇时，船已经开始下沉。）

grammar.explain: had better do 表「最好做某事」；否定 had better not do。易错点：had 无人称和数的变化。
examples: She had better put an address on its collar. （她最好给猫戴上地址项圈。）
You had better not inform the police. （你最好不要报警。）

tips: ①had done 表「过去的过去」；②常与 by the time、before 连用；③主线用一般过去时，更早一层用 had done。

title: Pioneer pilots
scene: 二十世纪初航空业尚处襁褓，巴西先驱桑托斯·杜蒙特在巴黎成功驾飞艇绕埃菲尔铁塔飞行，一九〇六年又完成欧洲有记录以来首次动力飞行。强风中很少有飞行员能操控轻薄的机器，他的勇气证明持续飞行已不再是梦。本课语法重点是 was/were able to 与 could 表过去能力。

words.eg: Santos-Dumont, a Brazilian pioneer, designed both balloons and powered aircraft.
The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.

words.cn: 不表同情的，无动于衷的
words.eg: But George was unsympathetic.
'That's Sebastian. You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.'
She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past.
Before they were replaced by steamships,
Workers had been restoring the old vessel for months when a sudden blaze broke out.
It marked the end of the great tradition of ships with sails and the beginning of a new era.
The first of the two ships to reach Java after the race had begun was the Thermopylae,
In August, she was struck by a very heavy storm during which her rudder was torn away.
rolled up his sleeves and pushed his right arm through the drain cover.
The Cutty Sark rolled from side to side and it became impossible to steer her.
A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks and it was fitted with great difficulty.
A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks and it was fitted with great difficulty.
for the simple reason that I was left with several curiously shaped bits of metal which did not seem to fit anywhere.

The strike has caused serious delays. 罢工已经造成严重延误。
for advertising exerts a subtle influence on us.

grammar.explain: was/were able to 表过去成功做到某事；could 表过去能力或可能。易错点：叙述「最终成功了」宜用 was able to。
examples: In Paris he was able to fly a small airship around the tower. （在巴黎他成功驾飞艇绕塔飞行。）
In 1906 he was able to make the first recorded powered flight. （一九〇六年他完成了有记录以来首次动力飞行。）

grammar.explain: could 表过去一般能力；与 was able to 相比，could 不强调是否成功。
examples: Few pilots could control such light machines in a strong wind. （很少有飞行员能在强风中操控如此轻的机器。）
He could spend money freely when he was famous. （成名时他可以随意挥霍。）

tips: ①was/were able to；②could 表过去能力。

title: Daniel Mendoza
scene: 丹尼尔·门多萨从一七六四年伦敦的贫寒出身，凭敏捷打法成为著名拳击手，击败众多强敌，后来开办拳击学校收入颇丰，却因挥霍多次入狱，晚年困苦但姓名从未被遗忘。本课语法重点是被动语态叙述成就与评价。

words.eg: Later he opened a boxing school and was well paid for teaching the wealthy.
However, he spent money freely and was imprisoned more than once for debt.
Much as we may pride ourselves on our good taste,
The industry learned that taste is personal and fame can disappear very quickly.
Antique shops exert a peculiar fascination on a great many people.
for advertising exerts a subtle influence on us.
The only funny things we heard that evening came from the advertiser at the beginning of the programme.
advertisers have made a close study of human nature and have classified all our little weaknesses.
An advertisement which begins with the magic word FREE can rarely go wrong.
These days, advertisers not only offer free samples,
They devise hundreds of competitions which will enable us to win huge sums of money.
Radio and television have made it possible for advertisers to capture the attention of millions of people in this way.
During a radio programme, a company of biscuit manufacturers once asked listeners to bake biscuits and send them to their factory.
One lady brought biscuit on a wheelbarrow. It weighed nearly 500 pounds.
A little later, a man came along with a biscuit which occupied the whole boot of his car.
What matters is the ingredient, not the price. 重要的是成分，不是价格。
It was so heavy that a crane had to be used to remove it from the lorry.
The manufacturers had to pay more money than they had anticipated, for they bought the biscuit from the student for $24, 000.
philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.
philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.

yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.
yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.

grammar.explain: 介绍建筑的历史与现状时，常用被动语态：历史事件用一般过去时被动（was founded），延续至今的情况用现在完成时被动（has been used）。易错点：found（创建）的过去分词是 founded，别与 find-found 混淆。
examples: The monastery was founded in the eleventh century. （这座修道院创建于十一世纪。）
A road has been built through the mountains. （一条公路已经修过了群山。）

tips: ①主语是承受者用被动 be + done；②时态体现在 be 上；③并列被动可对称省略。

title: By heart
scene: 演员在新戏中分到很长的角色，排练数周后确信句句烂熟，首演之夜却因两场戏顺序记反而一开口就想不起下一句，只好即兴发挥演完。导演说死记硬背与真正理解很难区分。本课语法重点是 be unaware of 与 tell the difference。

words.eg: philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.
philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.
yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.
yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.
In seeking independence, they do not sacrifice their human dignity.
He has deliberately chosen to lead the life he leads and is fully aware of the consequences.
What was the consequence of the editor's insistence on facts and statistics?
but he is free from the thousands of anxieties which afflict other people.
His few material possessions make it possible for him to move from place to place with ease.
advertisers have made a close study of human nature and have classified all our little weaknesses.
We often speak of tramps with contempt and put them in the same class as beggars,
but how many of us can honestly say that we have not felt a little envious of their simple way of life and their freedom from care?
Small boats loaded with wares sped to the great liner as she was entering the harbour.
Before she had anchored, the men from the boats had climbed on board
By the time the fire was brought under control, much of the upper deck had been destroyed.
silks from India, copper coffee pots, and beautiful handmade silverware.
The smell of roast chicken tempted her badly. 烤鸡的香味强烈地诱惑了她。
I felt especially pleased with my wonderful bargain--until I got back to the ship.
but I decided not to buy anything until I had disembarked.
I had no sooner got off the ship than I was assailed by a man who wanted to sell me a diamond ring.
Some of them were as big as marbles.
At the base of the gold cap, the words 'made in the U.S.A.' had been neatly inscribed.
The man said that the pen was worth ￡50, but as a special favour, he would let me have it for ￡30.
Gesticulating wildly, the man acted as if he found my offer outrageous,

words.cn: 出人预料的；令人不悦的
words.eg: Gesticulating wildly, the man acted as if he found my offer outrageous,

grammar.explain: be unaware of / be unaware that 表「没有意识到」。
examples: He walked on stage unaware that two scenes were in the wrong order. （他上台时没意识到两场戏顺序记反了。）
It was hard to tell memorizing from understanding. （死记硬背与真正理解很难区分。）

grammar.explain: tell the difference between A and B 表「区分 A 与 B」。易错点：difference 用单数。
examples: It was hard to tell memorizing from understanding. （死记硬背与真正理解很难区分。）
From that night on, he never confused learning by heart with knowing a role. （他再也不把背台词和懂角色混为一谈。）

title: One man's meat is another man's poison
scene: 电视节目请普通人讲述离奇经历，观众投票评选，无论故事多怪都看不厌；很快同类节目挤满各频道，一种套路让人厌倦——一个人的佳肴成了另一个人的毒药。本课语法重点是 no matter + 疑问词引导让步从句。

words.eg: What had been one man's meat became another man's poison overnight.
Soon similar programmes flooded every channel, and the format began to tire people.
The smell of roast chicken tempted her badly. 烤鸡的香味强烈地诱惑了她。
I felt especially pleased with my wonderful bargain--until I got back to the ship.
but I decided not to buy anything until I had disembarked.
I had no sooner got off the ship than I was assailed by a man who wanted to sell me a diamond ring.
Some of them were as big as marbles.
At the base of the gold cap, the words 'made in the U.S.A.' had been neatly inscribed.
The man said that the pen was worth ￡50, but as a special favour, he would let me have it for ￡30.
Gesticulating wildly, the man acted as if he found my offer outrageous,

words.cn: 出人预料的；令人不悦的
words.eg: Gesticulating wildly, the man acted as if he found my offer outrageous,
a moment later, he ran after me and thrust the pen into my hands.
Whether we find a joke funny or not largely depends on where we have been brought up.
Most funny stories are based on comic situations.
In spite of national differences, certain funny situations have a universal appeal.
That a joke is funny to some and not to others is clear to every comedian.
Many people find this sort of joke distasteful.
he kept on pestering his doctor to tell him when he would be able to go home.
We live in dread not only of unpleasant insects like spiders or wasps, but of quite harmless ones like moths.
Though the doctor did his best, the patient's recovery was slow.
On Christmas day, the man still had his right leg in plaster.
The following day, however, the doctor consoled him by telling him
The man took heart and sure enough, on New Year's Eve he was able to hobble along to a party.
To compensate for his unpleasant experiences in hospital,
He was still mumbling something about hospitals at the end of the party when he slipped on a piece of ice and broke his left leg.
Farm labourers said that they always woke up to find that work had been done overnight.

grammar.explain: no matter how/what/who 引导让步状语从句，表「无论……」。
examples: No matter how strange the stories were, audiences could not get enough. （无论故事多离奇，观众都看不厌。）
No matter what they offered, some viewers always switched channels. （无论推出什么，总有观众换台。）

title: A spot of bother
scene: 一位先生穿拖鞋倒垃圾，风把门带上，他没钥匙被锁在门外，尴尬之下叫消防队从窗户把他弄进屋；妻子说他根本不必叫他们，她有备用钥匙一小时内就会回来。本课语法重点是 needn't have done 与 didn't need to 的区别。

words.eg: On Christmas day, the man still had his right leg in plaster.
The following day, however, the doctor consoled him by telling him
The man took heart and sure enough, on New Year's Eve he was able to hobble along to a party.
To compensate for his unpleasant experiences in hospital,
He was still mumbling something about hospitals at the end of the party when he slipped on a piece of ice and broke his left leg.
Farm labourers said that they always woke up to find that work had been done overnight.
What had been one man's meat became another man's poison overnight.
Hay had been cut and cowsheds had been cleaned.
A farm worker, who stayed up all night, claimed to have seen a figure cutting corn in the moonlight.
A farm worker, who stayed up all night, claimed to have seen a figure cutting corn in the moonlight.
In time, it became an accepted fact that the Cox brothers employed a conscientious ghost that did most of their work for them.
At first she was the main suspect. 一开始她是主要嫌疑人。
As he hated army life, he decided to desert his regiment.
As he hated army life, he decided to desert his regiment.
His father told everybody that Eric had been killed in action.
All these years, Eric had lived as a recluse.
A lovable eccentric
A lovable eccentric
They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.
They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.
They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.
This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life.
This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life.
He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman,
He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.
He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.

grammar.explain: needn't have done 表「本不必做（但做了）」。易错点：与 didn't need to 不同。
examples: He needn't have called the fire brigade. （他本不必叫消防队。）
She said he needn't have called them at all. （她说他根本不必叫他们。）

grammar.explain: didn't need to do 表「不必做」，客观描述，不涉及是否实际做了。
examples: She didn't need to call anyone; she had a spare key. （她不必叫任何人，她有备用钥匙。）
We didn't need to get up early because it was a holiday. （假日不必早起。）

tips: ①needn't have done = 本不必做（但做了）；②didn't need to = 不必（可能没做）；③had better (not) do 表最好（不）做。
